titleEn: Presenting Greenhouse Grower’s 2026 Technology of the Year Finalists
titleZh: 2026年温室技术奖入围名单揭晓：空气托盘与PAR精准遮阳系统引领创新
summaryZh: 本文介绍了《温室种植者》杂志评选的2026年度技术奖两项入围技术。Air Tray Technologies通过空气修剪根系设计，替代传统圆形容器，促进纤维状根系生长，提高作物移栽后成活率、水分养分利用效率，适用于观赏植物、树木、柑橘及葡萄等多种园艺和农业作物。PARPerfect遮阳系统（Svensson）则实现了对光合有效辐射的动态精准控制，采用双层幕布配置，可全天连续调节遮阳率从20%至100%，优化光照管理。两项技术均聚焦于提升生产效率与作物品质，虽主要面向温室种植，但其根域环境优化与精准环境控制理念对智慧畜牧中的环境调控与自动化管理具有借鉴意义。
featuredReason: 智慧畜牧行业可借鉴其精准环境控制与根域优化思路，应用于养殖舍内微气候调控及动物福利提升，尤其是动态传感与自动化调节技术具有跨领域参考价值。
contentFull: By Brian D. Sparks|June 26, 2026 Greenhouse Grower will give out several awards at the Evening of Excellence during Cultivate’ 26 on July 13. Here’s your chance to get to know the finalists for this year’s Technology of the Year award before the winner is announced. Air Tray Technologies (Blackmore Co.) Air Tray Technologies gives growers a practical way to improve root quality, crop uniformity, and production efficiency without revamping growing system completely. It targets a core issue in our industry: inefficient root development due to continued use of the traditional round container which causes roots to circle. By encouraging air-pruned, fibrous root system, Air Tray helps plants establish faster after transplant, recover more consistently, and use water and nutrients more efficiently. By promoting air-pruned, fibrous, non-circling roots, Air Tray helps create a more efficient plant from the beginning of production. It improves oxygen exchange in the root zone, reduces overly wet conditions, and supports more consistent establishment after transplanting or shifting. The technology is also flexible, with multiple tray and container sizes that apply across horticultural and agricultural crops, including ornamentals, trees, shrubs, citrus, and viticulture. PARPerfect Shading System (Svensson) Top Articles Read More Read More Read More Read More Read More Reducing Common Greenhouse Injuries: Practical Steps to Protect Employees and Operations PARperfect advances greenhouse light management from fixed or reactive shading methods to dynamic, precision-based control of photosynthetically active radiation (PAR). It enables continuous adjustment of shading levels in response to changing outdoor radiation and crop requirements, and uses a dual screen configuration that pairs a highly diffusive Harmony PARperfect climate screen with a variable shading screen, allowing gradual control of screen positions from approximately 20% to 100% throughout the day. This design enables growers to stabilize PAR levels while avoiding spikes of direct sunlight that can cause crop stress, leaf damage, or uneven development. Over the past several years, adoption of PARperfect has supported improved light uniformity, higher usable cumulative light, and more consistent growing conditions, particularly in floriculture crops that are sensitive to light extremes. Pot-in-Tray Machine (BOLD Robotic Solutions) The Pot-In-Tray Machine is an efficient, reliable solution to one of the industry’s most repetitive, time-consuming tasks. By automating the task of loading pots into trays at high-speed, BOLD has helped greenhouses cut back on unproductive overhead and operational volatility. The Pot-In-Tray Machine’s compact design maximizes tight workspace efficiency without sacrificing power, delivering a high-speed output of up to 15 trays per minute. Fast modular changeovers allow for effortless transitions between sizes, creating a streamlined workflow that requires minimal manual intervention. The machine also offers an open-access design that simplifies maintenance and ensures the mechanics are always easy to reach and service. 1 1 5 Presenting Greenhouse Grower’s 2026 Technology of the Year Finalists Subscribe to eNewsletter Subscribe Brian D. Sparks is Editor of Greenhouse Grower and GreenhouseGrower.com. See all author stories here.
author: Brian Sparks
contentHtml:
Here’s your chance to get to know the finalists for this year’s Technology of the Year award before the winner is announced during Evening of Excellence at Cultivate’26.
The post Presenting Greenhouse Grower’s 2026 Technology of the Year Finalists appeared first on Greenhouse Grower.
